title: sub_GAME_7F062BE4: FP-weapon perspnorm word off-by-one (near arg 1.0 vs retail 0.0) + perspnorm/monitor-microcode command-order swap
repro: audit: asm_audit.py entry sub_GAME_7F062BE4; ASM gun.c:15010-15292 vs live C 14738-15006. RDP-stream: capture FP-weapon overlay DL, compare G_MOVEWORD perspnorm value (retail 436) and its position relative to the rocket-launcher monitor-microcode block

history.note: Two divergences re-derived from GLOBAL_ASM (US/JP block, retail 7F062BE4) vs live C. (D1) PERSPNORM ARG: retail computes the FP-weapon gSPPerspNormalize scale via sub_GAME_7F05997C(0.0f, 300.0f) — mtc1 $zero,$f12 at gun.c:15124 with f14=300.0 at 15120-15121 — giving 0x20000/300 = 436; live C gun.c:14888 passes (1.0f, 300.0f) giving 0x20000/301 = 435 (matrixmath.c:1300-1320 defines 7F05997C as u16(0x20000/(a+b))). Emitted moveword differs by 1 (0.23% vertex-precision scale on the first-person weapon). Nothing in comments marks the 1.0f intentional (the port's znear=1 rationale concerns its ADDED projection matrix, a separate documented substitution). (D2) COMMAND ORDER: retail emits the BC00000E perspnorm word BEFORE the numSwitches>=17 monitor-microcode block (emit at 15119-15128, process_monitor_animation_microcode receives the post-perspnorm cursor at 15163/15176); live C emits the monitor microcode FIRST (C 14843-14874) then perspnorm (14888) — rocket-launcher sight screen geometry therefore runs under the scene perspnorm instead of the weapon perspnorm. Rest of the retail block verified equivalent (lights group {19,18,2,3,20,21}, GBI words, envcolour byte order r<<24|g<<16|b<<8|a from 0xfdc-0xfdf, mirror-dual cullmode 3/2 by hand, rocket subdraw + firedrocket clear, tail viGetPerspNorm + WATCHLASER 7F061E18). DO NOT fix from this note; re-derive from ASM. Fix default-ON w/ GE007_NO_* control; verify via RDP-command diff on the FP overlay.
